questions.question: What two knobs split a fixed training-compute budget?
options: Parameters N and training tokens D, with FLOPs ≈ 6·N·D — you can trade a bigger model on less data against a smaller model on more data
questions.explanation: Pre-2022 the answer was "push N hard" — GPT-3 was 175B params on ~300B tokens (1.7 tokens/param), backed by the Kaplan laws.

questions.question: What did Chinchilla (Hoffmann 2022) discover?
options: The compute-optimal ratio is ~20 tokens per parameter — GPT-3 was 10x undertrained; Chinchilla (70B, 1.4T tokens) beat GPT-3 (175B, 300B) on every benchmark at 2.5x lower inference cost
questions.explanation: From L(N,D) = A/N^α + B/D^β + E, taking the derivative at fixed compute gives D_opt/N_opt ≈ 20 — the result that reorganized frontier training.

questions.question: What do the terms in L(N,D) = A/N^α + B/D^β + E represent?
options: A capacity-limited term shrinking with parameters (α≈0.34), a data-limited term shrinking with tokens (β≈0.28), and E≈1.69 — the IRREDUCIBLE loss floor no scale removes
questions.explanation: The two power-law terms trade against each other; near-symmetric exponents are why the optimal compute split is roughly even between model size and data.

questions.question: Why does 2026 deliberately train PAST Chinchilla-optimal?
options: Chinchilla optimizes training loss per training FLOP, but training is paid once and inference forever — Llama 3 8B trained on 15T tokens (1,875 tokens/param, 94x past optimal) buys a smaller deployable footprint at equal quality
To use up idle GPU clusters
questions.explanation: The objective changed: not "best model per training dollar" but "cheapest-to-serve model at a quality bar." Over-training small models is the deployment-economics answer.

questions.question: What practical discipline do scaling laws give a training run?
options: They guarantee any run will succeed

Fit the law on small cheap runs, then PREDICT the loss of the expensive run before launching — budget misallocations (too big a model, too little data) become visible on paper instead of after a multi-million-dollar training job
questions.explanation: That predictability is the point: scaling laws turned frontier training from artisanal gambling into capacity planning.
